revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称=庚申塚古墳 |画像= |所在地=[[長野県]][[飯綱町]]大字平出字西浦1007-1、1008-1 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|43|22|N|138|15|01|E|scale:20000}} |形状=[[前方後円墳]] |築造年代=5世紀前半 |規模=全長約52[[メートル|m]]、後円部径約31メートル |被葬者=不明 |出土品=格子目叩きの [[埴輪]]（[[円筒埴輪]]） |史跡指定=[[1980年]]（[[昭和]]55年）町（旧村）指定}}
'''庚申塚古墳'''（こうしんづかこふん）とは、[[長野県]][[飯綱町]]平出にある[[前方後円墳]]。
古くから塚の上に[[庚申塔]]が祀られていたので庚申塚と呼ばれてきた。 [[1933年]]（[[昭和]]8年）に栗岩英治が庚申塚を調査して、前方後円墳であるとした。
[[古墳時代]]（5世紀前半）築造されたと考えられる古墳で、国内6例目となる格子目叩きが施された [[埴輪]]（[[円筒埴輪]]）が出土した。
== 概要 == *墳形：[[前方後円墳]] *規模 **全長 約52[[メートル]] **後円部径 約31メートル **後円部高さ 3.5メートル **前方部の先端の幅 約15～17メートル **前方部高さ 1.5メートル
== 所在地 == *長野県上水内郡飯綱町大字平出字西浦1007-1、1008-1
== 交通アクセス == *最寄駅：[[しなの鉄道]][[牟礼駅]]（車約8分）
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == *[[丹霞郷]] *[[日本の古墳一覧]]
== 参考文献 == * 牟礼村誌・学校誌編纂委員会『牟礼村誌』上巻、419-420、817頁。
{{デフォルトソート:こうしんつかこふん}} [[Category:長野県の古墳]] [[Category:飯綱町の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]]

[[古墳時代]]（5世紀前半）築造されたと考えられる古墳で、国内6例目となる格子目叩きが施された [[埴輪]]（[[円筒埴輪]]）が出土した。

古くから塚の上に[[庚申塔]]が祀られていたので庚申塚と呼ばれてきた。 [[1933年]]（[[昭和]]8年）に栗岩英治が庚申塚を調査して、前方後円墳であるとした。
[[古墳時代]]（5世紀前半）築造されたと考えられる古墳で、国内6例目となる格子目叩きが施された [[埴輪]]（[[円筒埴輪]]）が出土した。

revision.text:
{{Multiple Issues | Refs Needed = June 2023 | Single Source = June 2023 }} {{Ancient Japanese Burial Mounds |Name = Kōshinzuka Kofun |Image = [[File:庚申塚古墳１.jpg|250px|Full view of Kōshinzuka Kofun]] |Address = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}{{ill|Iizuna Town|ja|飯綱町}} Hirade, Nishinura 1007-1, 1008-1 |Location = {{coord|36|43|22|N|138|15|01|E|scale:20000}} |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} |Construction Period = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Dimensions = Total length: approx. 52m; Rear circle diameter: approx. 31m |Buried Person = Unknown |Artifacts = Gridded {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} ({{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}) |Historic Site Designation = Town (formerly village) designated in [[1980]] (Showa 55) }} '''Kōshinzuka Kofun''' (庚申塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Iizuna Town|ja|飯綱町}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}.
The mound is named "Kōshinzuka" because an ancient {{ill|Kōshin-tō|ja|庚申塔}} was enshrined on it. In [[1933]] (Showa 8), Eiji Kuriiwa conducted an investigation and identified the site as a keyhole-shaped burial mound.
The mound, believed to have been constructed in the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, yielded artifacts including gridded {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (cylindrical Haniwa), marking it as the sixth example of such finds in Japan.
== Overview ==
* Shape: {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} * Dimensions: * Total length: approx. 52m * Rear circle diameter: approx. 31m * Rear circle height: 3.5m * Front projection width: approx. 15–17m * Front projection height: 1.5m
<gallery widths="200px"> 庚申塚古墳２.jpg|Close view of Kōshinzuka Kofun 庚申塚古墳３.jpg|Stone monument 庚申塚古墳４.jpg|Kōshin-tō </gallery>
== Location ==
* Nagano Prefecture, Kamiminochi District, {{ill|Iizuna Town|ja|飯綱町}}, Hirade, Nishinura 1007-1, 1008-1
== Transportation Access ==
* Nearest Station: {{ill|Shinano Railway|ja|しなの鉄道}} {{ill|Mure Station|ja|牟礼駅}} (Approx. 8 minutes by car)
== References ==
* Mure Village History and School History Compilation Committee, *Mure Village History* (Vol. 1), pp. 419–420, 817.
== Related Topics ==
* {{ill|Tankakyō|ja|丹霞郷}} * {{ill|List of Burial Mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iizuna Town]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped burial mounds]] [[ja:庚申塚古墳]]

{{Multiple Issues | Refs Needed = June 2023 | Single Source = June 2023 }} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kōshinzuka Kofun |image = [[File:庚申塚古墳１.jpg|250px|Full view of Kōshinzuka Kofun]] |Address = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}{{ill|Iizuna Town|ja|飯綱町}} Hirade, Nishinura 1007-1, 1008-1 |location = {{coord|36|43|22|N|138|15|01|E|scale:20000}} |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} |Construction Period = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Dimensions = Total length: approx. 52m; Rear circle diameter: approx. 31m |Buried Person = Unknown |Artifacts = Gridded {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} ({{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}) |Historic Site Designation = Town (formerly village) designated in [[1980]] (Showa 55) }} '''Kōshinzuka Kofun''' (庚申塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Iizuna Town|ja|飯綱町}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}.
The mound is named "Kōshinzuka" because an ancient {{ill|Kōshin-tō|ja|庚申塔}} was enshrined on it. In [[1933]] (Showa 8), Eiji Kuriiwa conducted an investigation and identified the site as a keyhole-shaped burial mound.
The mound, believed to have been constructed in the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, yielded artifacts including gridded {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (cylindrical Haniwa), marking it as the sixth example of such finds in Japan.
== Overview ==
* Shape: {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} * Dimensions: * Total length: approx. 52m * Rear circle diameter: approx. 31m * Rear circle height: 3.5m * Front projection width: approx. 15–17m * Front projection height: 1.5m
<gallery widths="200px"> 庚申塚古墳２.jpg|Close view of Kōshinzuka Kofun 庚申塚古墳３.jpg|Stone monument 庚申塚古墳４.jpg|Kōshin-tō </gallery>
== location ==
* Nagano Prefecture, Kamiminochi District, {{ill|Iizuna Town|ja|飯綱町}}, Hirade, Nishinura 1007-1, 1008-1
== Transportation Access ==
* Nearest Station: {{ill|Shinano Railway|ja|しなの鉄道}} {{ill|Mure Station|ja|牟礼駅}} (Approx. 8 minutes by car)
== References ==
* Mure Village History and School History Compilation Committee, *Mure Village History* (Vol. 1), pp. 419–420, 817.
== Related Topics ==
* {{ill|Tankakyō|ja|丹霞郷}} * {{ill|List of Burial Mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}

{{Multiple Issues | Refs Needed = June 2023 | Single Source = June 2023 }} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kōshinzuka Kofun |image = [[File:庚申塚古墳１.jpg|250px|Full view of Kōshinzuka Kofun]] |Address = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}{{ill|Iizuna Town|ja|飯綱町}} Hirade, Nishinura 1007-1, 1008-1 |location = {{coord|36|43|22|N|138|15|01|E|scale:20000}} |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} |Construction Period = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Dimensions = Total length: approx. 52m; Rear circle diameter: approx. 31m |Buried Person = Unknown |Artifacts = Gridded {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} ({{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}) |Historic Site Designation = Town (formerly village) designated in [[1980]] (Showa 55) }} '''Kōshinzuka Kofun''' (庚申塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Iizuna Town|ja|飯綱町}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}.
The mound is named "Kōshinzuka" because an ancient {{ill|Kōshin-tō|ja|庚申塔}} was enshrined on it. In [[1933]] (Showa 8), Eiji Kuriiwa conducted an investigation and identified the site as a keyhole-shaped burial mound.
The mound, believed to have been constructed in the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, yielded artifacts including gridded {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (cylindrical Haniwa), marking it as the sixth example of such finds in Japan.
== Overview ==
* Shape: {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} * Dimensions: * Total length: approx. 52m * Rear circle diameter: approx. 31m * Rear circle height: 3.5m * Front projection width: approx. 15–17m * Front projection height: 1.5m
<gallery widths="200px"> 庚申塚古墳２.jpg|Close view of Kōshinzuka Kofun 庚申塚古墳３.jpg|Stone monument 庚申塚古墳４.jpg|Kōshin-tō </gallery>
== location ==
* Nagano Prefecture, Kamiminochi District, {{ill|Iizuna Town|ja|飯綱町}}, Hirade, Nishinura 1007-1, 1008-1
== Transportation Access ==
* Nearest Station: {{ill|Shinano Railway|ja|しなの鉄道}} {{ill|Mure Station|ja|牟礼駅}} (Approx. 8 minutes by car)
== References ==
* Mure Village History and School History Compilation Committee, *Mure Village History* (Vol. 1), pp. 419–420, 817.
== Related Topics ==
* {{ill|Tankakyō|ja|丹霞郷}} * {{ill|List of Burial Mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}

revision.text:
{{Multiple Issues | Refs Needed = June 2023 | Single Source = June 2023 }} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kōshinzuka Kofun |image = [[File:庚申塚古墳１.jpg|250px|Full view of Kōshinzuka Kofun]] |Address = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}{{ill|Iizuna Town|ja|飯綱町}} Hirade, Nishinura 1007-1, 1008-1 |location = {{coord|36|43|22|N|138|15|01|E|scale:20000}} |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} |Construction Period = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Dimensions = Total length: approx. 52m; Rear circle diameter: approx. 31m |Buried Person = Unknown |Artifacts = Gridded {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} ({{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}) |Historic Site Designation = Town (formerly village) designated in [[1980]] (Showa 55) }} '''Kōshinzuka Kofun''' (庚申塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Iizuna Town|ja|飯綱町}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}.
The mound is named "Kōshinzuka" because an ancient {{ill|Kōshin-tō|ja|庚申塔}} was enshrined on it. In [[1933]] (Showa 8), Eiji Kuriiwa conducted an investigation and identified the site as a keyhole-shaped burial mound.
The mound, believed to have been constructed in the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, yielded artifacts including gridded {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (cylindrical Haniwa), marking it as the sixth example of such finds in Japan.
== Overview ==
* Shape: {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} * Dimensions: * Total length: approx. 52m * Rear circle diameter: approx. 31m * Rear circle height: 3.5m * Front projection width: approx. 15–17m * Front projection height: 1.5m
<gallery widths="200px"> 庚申塚古墳２.jpg|Close view of Kōshinzuka Kofun 庚申塚古墳３.jpg|Stone monument 庚申塚古墳４.jpg|Kōshin-tō </gallery>
== location ==
* Nagano Prefecture, Kamiminochi District, {{ill|Iizuna Town|ja|飯綱町}}, Hirade, Nishinura 1007-1, 1008-1
== Transportation Access ==
* Nearest Station: {{ill|Shinano Railway|ja|しなの鉄道}} {{ill|Mure Station|ja|牟礼駅}} (Approx. 8 minutes by car)
== References ==
* Mure Village History and School History Compilation Committee, *Mure Village History* (Vol. 1), pp. 419–420, 817.
== Related Topics ==
* {{ill|Tankakyō|ja|丹霞郷}} * {{ill|List of Burial Mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}

The mound, believed to have been constructed in the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, yielded artifacts including gridded {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (cylindrical Haniwa), marking it as the sixth example of such finds in Japan.
== Overview ==
* Shape: {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} * Dimensions: * Total length: approx. 52m * Rear circle diameter: approx. 31m * Rear circle height: 3.5m * Front projection width: approx. 15–17m * Front projection height: 1.5m
<gallery widths="200px"> 庚申塚古墳２.jpg|Close view of Kōshinzuka Kofun 庚申塚古墳３.jpg|Stone monument 庚申塚古墳４.jpg|Kōshin-tō </gallery>

== Transportation Access ==
* Nearest Station: {{ill|Shinano Railway|ja|しなの鉄道}} {{ill|Mure Station|ja|牟礼駅}} (Approx. 8 minutes by car)
== References ==
* Mure Village History and School History Compilation Committee, *Mure Village History* (Vol. 1), pp. 419–420, 817.
== Related Topics ==
* {{ill|Tankakyō|ja|丹霞郷}} * {{ill|List of Burial Mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}

{{Multiple Issues | Refs Needed = June 2023 | Single Source = June 2023 }} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kōshinzuka Kofun |image = [[File:庚申塚古墳１.jpg|250px|Full view of Kōshinzuka Kofun]] |Address = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}{{ill|Iizuna Town|ja|飯綱町}} Hirade, Nishinura 1007-1, 1008-1 |location = {{coord|36|43|22|N|138|15|01|E|scale:20000}} |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} |Construction Period = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Dimensions = Total length: approx. 52m; Rear circle diameter: approx. 31m |Buried Person = Unknown |Artifacts = Gridded {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} ({{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}) |Historic Site Designation = Town (formerly village) designated in [[1980]] (Showa 55) }} '''Kōshinzuka Kofun''' (庚申塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Iizuna Town|ja|飯綱町}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}.
The mound is named "Kōshinzuka" because an ancient {{ill|Kōshin-tō|ja|庚申塔}} was enshrined on it. In [[1933]] (Showa 8), Eiji Kuriiwa conducted an investigation and identified the site as a keyhole-shaped burial mound.
The mound, believed to have been constructed in the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, yielded artifacts including gridded {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (cylindrical Haniwa), marking it as the sixth example of such finds in Japan.
== Overview ==
* Shape: {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} * Dimensions: * Total length: approx. 52m * Rear circle diameter: approx. 31m * Rear circle height: 3.5m * Front projection width: approx. 15–17m * Front projection height: 1.5m
<gallery widths="200px"> 庚申塚古墳２.jpg|Close view of Kōshinzuka Kofun 庚申塚古墳３.jpg|Stone monument 庚申塚古墳４.jpg|Kōshin-tō </gallery>
== location ==
* Nagano Prefecture, Kamiminochi District, {{ill|Iizuna Town|ja|飯綱町}}, Hirade, Nishinura 1007-1, 1008-1
== Transportation Access ==
* Nearest Station: {{ill|Shinano Railway|ja|しなの鉄道}} {{ill|Mure Station|ja|牟礼駅}} (Approx. 8 minutes by car)
== References ==
* Mure Village History and School History Compilation Committee, *Mure Village History* (Vol. 1), pp. 419–420, 817.
== Related Topics ==
* {{ill|Tankakyō|ja|丹霞郷}} * {{ill|List of Burial Mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}

revision.text:
{{Multiple Issues | Refs Needed = June 2023 | Single Source = June 2023 }} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kōshinzuka Kofun |image = [[File:庚申塚古墳１.jpg|250px|Full view of Kōshinzuka Kofun]] |Address = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}{{ill|Iizuna Town|ja|飯綱町}} Hirade, Nishinura 1007-1, 1008-1 |location = {{coord|36|43|22|N|138|15|01|E|scale:20000}} |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} |Construction Period = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Dimensions = Total length: approx. 52m; Rear circle diameter: approx. 31m |Buried Person = Unknown |Artifacts = Gridded {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} ({{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}) |Historic Site Designation = Town (formerly village) designated in [[1980]] (Showa 55) }} '''Kōshinzuka Kofun''' (庚申塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Iizuna Town|ja|飯綱町}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}.
The mound is named "Kōshinzuka" because an ancient {{ill|Kōshin-tō|ja|庚申塔}} was enshrined on it. In [[1933]] (Showa 8), Eiji Kuriiwa conducted an investigation and identified the site as a keyhole-shaped burial mound.
The mound, believed to have been constructed in the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, yielded artifacts including gridded {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (cylindrical Haniwa), marking it as the sixth example of such finds in Japan.
== Overview ==
* Shape: {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} * Dimensions: * Total length: approx. 52m * Rear circle diameter: approx. 31m * Rear circle height: 3.5m * Front projection width: approx. 15–17m * Front projection height: 1.5m
<gallery widths="200px"> 庚申塚古墳２.jpg|Close view of Kōshinzuka Kofun 庚申塚古墳３.jpg|Stone monument 庚申塚古墳４.jpg|Kōshin-tō </gallery>
== location ==
* Nagano Prefecture, Kamiminochi District, {{ill|Iizuna Town|ja|飯綱町}}, Hirade, Nishinura 1007-1, 1008-1
== Transportation Access ==
* Nearest Station: {{ill|Shinano Railway|ja|しなの鉄道}} {{ill|Mure Station|ja|牟礼駅}} (Approx. 8 minutes by car)
== References ==
* Mure Village History and School History Compilation Committee, *Mure Village History* (Vol. 1), pp. 419–420, 817.
== Related Topics ==
* {{ill|Tankakyō|ja|丹霞郷}} * {{ill|List of Burial Mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}

{{Multiple Issues | Refs Needed = June 2023 | Single Source = June 2023 }} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kōshinzuka Kofun |image = [[File:庚申塚古墳１.jpg|250px|Full view of Kōshinzuka Kofun]] |Address = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}{{ill|Iizuna Town|ja|飯綱町}} Hirade, Nishinura 1007-1, 1008-1 |location = {{coord|36|43|22|N|138|15|01|E|scale:20000}} |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} |Construction Period = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Dimensions = Total length: approx. 52m; Rear circle diameter: approx. 31m |Buried Person = Unknown |Artifacts = Gridded {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} ({{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}) |Historic Site Designation = Town (formerly village) designated in [[1980]] (Showa 55) }} '''Kōshinzuka Kofun''' (庚申塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Iizuna Town|ja|飯綱町}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}.
The mound is named "Kōshinzuka" because an ancient {{ill|Kōshin-tō|ja|庚申塔}} was enshrined on it. In [[1933]] (Showa 8), Eiji Kuriiwa conducted an investigation and identified the site as a keyhole-shaped burial mound.
The mound, believed to have been constructed in the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, yielded artifacts including gridded {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (cylindrical Haniwa), marking it as the sixth example of such finds in Japan.
== Overview ==
* Shape: {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} * Dimensions: * Total length: approx. 52m * Rear circle diameter: approx. 31m * Rear circle height: 3.5m * Front projection width: approx. 15–17m * Front projection height: 1.5m
<gallery widths="200px"> 庚申塚古墳２.jpg|Close view of Kōshinzuka Kofun 庚申塚古墳３.jpg|Stone monument 庚申塚古墳４.jpg|Kōshin-tō </gallery>
== location ==
* Nagano Prefecture, Kamiminochi District, {{ill|Iizuna Town|ja|飯綱町}}, Hirade, Nishinura 1007-1, 1008-1
== Transportation Access ==
* Nearest Station: {{ill|Shinano Railway|ja|しなの鉄道}} {{ill|Mure Station|ja|牟礼駅}} (Approx. 8 minutes by car)
== References ==
* Mure Village History and School History Compilation Committee, *Mure Village History* (Vol. 1), pp. 419–420, 817.
== Related Topics ==
* {{ill|Tankakyō|ja|丹霞郷}} * {{ill|List of Burial Mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後円墳]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Multiple Issues | Refs Needed = June 2023 | Single Source = June 2023 }} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kōshinzuka Kofun |image = [[File:庚申塚古墳１.jpg|250px|Full view of Kōshinzuka Kofun]] |Address = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}{{ill|Iizuna Town|ja|飯綱町}} Hirade, Nishinura 1007-1, 1008-1 |location = {{coord|36|43|22|N|138|15|01|E|scale:20000}} |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} |Construction Period = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Dimensions = Total length: approx. 52m; Rear circle diameter: approx. 31m |Buried Person = Unknown |Artifacts = Gridded {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} ({{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}) |Historic Site Designation = Town (formerly village) designated in [[1980]] (Showa 55) }} '''Kōshinzuka Kofun''' (庚申塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Iizuna Town|ja|飯綱町}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}.
The mound is named "Kōshinzuka" because an ancient {{ill|Kōshin-tō|ja|庚申塔}} was enshrined on it. In [[1933]] (Showa 8), Eiji Kuriiwa conducted an investigation and identified the site as a keyhole-shaped burial mound.
The mound, believed to have been constructed in the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, yielded artifacts including gridded {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (cylindrical Haniwa), marking it as the sixth example of such finds in Japan.
== Overview ==
* Shape: {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} * Dimensions: * Total length: approx. 52m * Rear circle diameter: approx. 31m * Rear circle height: 3.5m * Front projection width: approx. 15–17m * Front projection height: 1.5m
<gallery widths="200px"> 庚申塚古墳２.jpg|Close view of Kōshinzuka Kofun 庚申塚古墳３.jpg|Stone monument 庚申塚古墳４.jpg|Kōshin-tō </gallery>
== location ==
* Nagano Prefecture, Kamiminochi District, {{ill|Iizuna Town|ja|飯綱町}}, Hirade, Nishinura 1007-1, 1008-1
== Transportation Access ==
* Nearest Station: {{ill|Shinano Railway|ja|しなの鉄道}} {{ill|Mure Station|ja|牟礼駅}} (Approx. 8 minutes by car)
== References ==
* Mure Village History and School History Compilation Committee, *Mure Village History* (Vol. 1), pp. 419–420, 817.
== Related Topics ==
* {{ill|Tankakyō|ja|丹霞郷}} * {{ill|List of Burial Mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後円墳]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Multiple Issues | Refs Needed = June 2023 | Single Source = June 2023 }} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kōshinzuka Kofun |image = [[File:庚申塚古墳１.jpg|250px|Full view of Kōshinzuka Kofun]] |Address = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}{{ill|Iizuna Town|ja|飯綱町}} Hirade, Nishinura 1007-1, 1008-1 |location = {{coord|36|43|22|N|138|15|01|E|scale:20000}} |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} |Construction Period = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Dimensions = Total length: approx. 52m; Rear circle diameter: approx. 31m |Buried Person = Unknown |Artifacts = Gridded {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} ({{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}) |Historic Site Designation = Town (formerly village) designated in [[1980]] (Showa 55) }} '''Kōshinzuka Kofun''' (庚申塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Iizuna Town|ja|飯綱町}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}.
The mound is named "Kōshinzuka" because an ancient {{ill|Kōshin-tō|ja|庚申塔}} was enshrined on it. In [[1933]] (Showa 8), Eiji Kuriiwa conducted an investigation and identified the site as a keyhole-shaped burial mound.
The mound, believed to have been constructed in the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, yielded artifacts including gridded {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (cylindrical Haniwa), marking it as the sixth example of such finds in Japan.
== Overview ==
* Shape: {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} * Dimensions: * Total length: approx. 52m * Rear circle diameter: approx. 31m * Rear circle height: 3.5m * Front projection width: approx. 15–17m * Front projection height: 1.5m
<gallery widths="200px"> 庚申塚古墳２.jpg|Close view of Kōshinzuka Kofun 庚申塚古墳３.jpg|Stone monument 庚申塚古墳４.jpg|Kōshin-tō </gallery>
== location ==
* Nagano Prefecture, Kamiminochi District, {{ill|Iizuna Town|ja|飯綱町}}, Hirade, Nishinura 1007-1, 1008-1
== Transportation Access ==
* Nearest Station: {{ill|Shinano Railway|ja|しなの鉄道}} {{ill|Mure Station|ja|牟礼駅}} (Approx. 8 minutes by car)
== References ==
* Mure Village History and School History Compilation Committee, *Mure Village History* (Vol. 1), pp. 419–420, 817.
== Related Topics ==
* {{ill|Tankakyō|ja|丹霞郷}} * {{ill|List of Burial Mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後円墳]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Multiple Issues | Refs Needed = June 2023 | Single Source = June 2023 }} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kōshinzuka Kofun |image = [[File:庚申塚古墳１.jpg|250px|Full view of Kōshinzuka Kofun]] |Address = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}{{ill|Iizuna Town|ja|飯綱町}} Hirade, Nishinura 1007-1, 1008-1 |location = {{coord|36|43|22|N|138|15|01|E|scale:20000}} |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} |Construction Period = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Dimensions = Total length: approx. 52m; Rear circle diameter: approx. 31m |Buried Person = Unknown |Artifacts = Gridded {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} ({{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}) |Historic Site Designation = Town (formerly village) designated in [[1980]] (Showa 55) }} '''Kōshinzuka Kofun''' (庚申塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Iizuna Town|ja|飯綱町}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}.
The mound is named "Kōshinzuka" because an ancient {{ill|Kōshin-tō|ja|庚申塔}} was enshrined on it. In [[1933]] (Showa 8), Eiji Kuriiwa conducted an investigation and identified the site as a keyhole-shaped burial mound.
The mound, believed to have been constructed in the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, yielded artifacts including gridded {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (cylindrical Haniwa), marking it as the sixth example of such finds in Japan.
== Overview ==
* Shape: {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} * Dimensions: * Total length: approx. 52m * Rear circle diameter: approx. 31m * Rear circle height: 3.5m * Front projection width: approx. 15–17m * Front projection height: 1.5m
<gallery widths="200px"> 庚申塚古墳２.jpg|Close view of Kōshinzuka Kofun 庚申塚古墳３.jpg|Stone monument 庚申塚古墳４.jpg|Kōshin-tō </gallery>
== location ==
* Nagano Prefecture, Kamiminochi District, {{ill|Iizuna Town|ja|飯綱町}}, Hirade, Nishinura 1007-1, 1008-1
== Transportation Access ==
* Nearest Station: {{ill|Shinano Railway|ja|しなの鉄道}} {{ill|Mure Station|ja|牟礼駅}} (Approx. 8 minutes by car)
== References ==
* Mure Village History and School History Compilation Committee, *Mure Village History* (Vol. 1), pp. 419–420, 817.
== Related Topics ==
* {{ill|Tankakyō|ja|丹霞郷}} * {{ill|List of Burial Mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後円墳]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Multiple Issues | Refs Needed = June 2023 | Single Source = June 2023 }} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kōshinzuka Kofun |image = [[File:庚申塚古墳１.jpg|250px|Full view of Kōshinzuka Kofun]] |Address = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}{{ill|Iizuna Town|ja|飯綱町}} Hirade, Nishinura 1007-1, 1008-1 |location = {{coord|36|43|22|N|138|15|01|E|scale:20000}} |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} |Construction Period = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Dimensions = Total length: approx. 52m; Rear circle diameter: approx. 31m |Buried Person = Unknown |Artifacts = Gridded {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} ({{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}) |Historic Site Designation = Town (formerly village) designated in [[1980]] (Showa 55) }} '''Kōshinzuka Kofun''' (庚申塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Iizuna Town|ja|飯綱町}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}.
The mound is named "Kōshinzuka" because an ancient {{ill|Kōshin-tō|ja|庚申塔}} was enshrined on it. In [[1933]] (Showa 8), Eiji Kuriiwa conducted an investigation and identified the site as a keyhole-shaped burial mound.
The mound, believed to have been constructed in the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, yielded artifacts including gridded {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (cylindrical Haniwa), marking it as the sixth example of such finds in Japan.
== Overview ==
* Shape: {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} * Dimensions: * Total length: approx. 52m * Rear circle diameter: approx. 31m * Rear circle height: 3.5m * Front projection width: approx. 15–17m * Front projection height: 1.5m
<gallery widths="200px"> 庚申塚古墳２.jpg|Close view of Kōshinzuka Kofun 庚申塚古墳３.jpg|Stone monument 庚申塚古墳４.jpg|Kōshin-tō </gallery>
== location ==
* Nagano Prefecture, Kamiminochi District, {{ill|Iizuna Town|ja|飯綱町}}, Hirade, Nishinura 1007-1, 1008-1
== Transportation Access ==
* Nearest Station: {{ill|Shinano Railway|ja|しなの鉄道}} {{ill|Mure Station|ja|牟礼駅}} (Approx. 8 minutes by car)
== References ==
* Mure Village History and School History Compilation Committee, *Mure Village History* (Vol. 1), pp. 419–420, 817.
== Related Topics ==
* {{ill|Tankakyō|ja|丹霞郷}} * {{ill|List of Burial Mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}

* Shape: {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} * Dimensions: * Total length: approx. 52m * Rear circle diameter: approx. 31m * Rear circle height: 3.5m * Front projection width: approx. 15–17m * Front projection height: 1.5m

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後円墳]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Multiple Issues | Refs Needed = June 2023 | Single Source = June 2023 }} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kōshinzuka Kofun |image = [[File:庚申塚古墳１.jpg|250px|Full view of Kōshinzuka Kofun]] |Address = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}{{ill|Iizuna Town|ja|飯綱町}} Hirade, Nishinura 1007-1, 1008-1 |location = {{coord|36|43|22|N|138|15|01|E|scale:20000}} |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} |Construction Period = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Dimensions = Total length: approx. 52m; Rear circle diameter: approx. 31m |Buried Person = Unknown |Artifacts = Gridded {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} ({{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}) |Historic Site Designation = Town (formerly village) designated in [[1980]] (Showa 55) }} '''Kōshinzuka Kofun''' (庚申塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Iizuna Town|ja|飯綱町}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}.
The mound is named "Kōshinzuka" because an ancient {{ill|Kōshin-tō|ja|庚申塔}} was enshrined on it. In [[1933]] (Showa 8), Eiji Kuriiwa conducted an investigation and identified the site as a keyhole-shaped burial mound.
The mound, believed to have been constructed in the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, yielded artifacts including gridded {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (cylindrical Haniwa), marking it as the sixth example of such finds in Japan.
== Overview ==
* Shape: {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} * Dimensions: * Total length: approx. 52m * Rear circle diameter: approx. 31m * Rear circle height: 3.5m * Front projection width: approx. 15–17m * Front projection height: 1.5m
<gallery widths="200px"> 庚申塚古墳２.jpg|Close view of Kōshinzuka Kofun 庚申塚古墳３.jpg|Stone monument 庚申塚古墳４.jpg|Kōshin-tō </gallery>
== location ==
* Nagano Prefecture, Kamiminochi District, {{ill|Iizuna Town|ja|飯綱町}}, Hirade, Nishinura 1007-1, 1008-1
== Transportation Access ==
* Nearest Station: {{ill|Shinano Railway|ja|しなの鉄道}} {{ill|Mure Station|ja|牟礼駅}} (Approx. 8 minutes by car)
== References ==
* Mure Village History and School History Compilation Committee, *Mure Village History* (Vol. 1), pp. 419–420, 817.
== Related Topics ==
* {{ill|Tankakyō|ja|丹霞郷}} * {{ill|List of Burial Mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}

revision.text:
{{Multiple Issues | Refs Needed = June 2023 | Single Source = June 2023 }} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kōshinzuka Kofun |image = [[File:庚申塚古墳１.jpg|250px|Full view of Kōshinzuka Kofun]] |Address = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}{{ill|Iizuna Town|ja|飯綱町}} Hirade, Nishinura 1007-1, 1008-1 |location = {{coord|36|43|22|N|138|15|01|E|scale:20000}} |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |Construction Period = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Dimensions = Total length: approx. 52m; Rear circle diameter: approx. 31m |Buried Person = Unknown |Artifacts = Gridded {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} ({{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}) |Historic Site Designation = Town (formerly village) designated in [[1980]] (Showa 55) }} '''Kōshinzuka Kofun''' (庚申塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Iizuna Town|ja|飯綱町}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}.
The mound is named "Kōshinzuka" because an ancient {{ill|Kōshin-tō|ja|庚申塔}} was enshrined on it. In [[1933]] (Showa 8), Eiji Kuriiwa conducted an investigation and identified the site as a keyhole-shaped burial mound.
The mound, believed to have been constructed in the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, yielded artifacts including gridded {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (cylindrical Haniwa), marking it as the sixth example of such finds in Japan.
== Overview ==
* Shape: {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} * Dimensions: * Total length: approx. 52m * Rear circle diameter: approx. 31m * Rear circle height: 3.5m * Front projection width: approx. 15–17m * Front projection height: 1.5m
<gallery widths="200px"> 庚申塚古墳２.jpg|Close view of Kōshinzuka Kofun 庚申塚古墳３.jpg|Stone monument 庚申塚古墳４.jpg|Kōshin-tō </gallery>
== location ==
* Nagano Prefecture, Kamiminochi District, {{ill|Iizuna Town|ja|飯綱町}}, Hirade, Nishinura 1007-1, 1008-1
== Transportation Access ==
* Nearest Station: {{ill|Shinano Railway|ja|しなの鉄道}} {{ill|Mure Station|ja|牟礼駅}} (Approx. 8 minutes by car)
== References ==
* Mure Village History and School History Compilation Committee, *Mure Village History* (Vol. 1), pp. 419–420, 817.
== Related Topics ==
* {{ill|Tankakyō|ja|丹霞郷}} * {{ill|List of Burial Mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}

revision.comment: mass eplacemen
revision.text:
{{Multiple Issues | Refs Needed = June 2023 | Single Source = June 2023 }} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kōshinzuka Kofun |image = [[File:庚申塚古墳１.jpg|250px|Full view of Kōshinzuka Kofun]] |Address = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}{{ill|Iizuna Town|ja|飯綱町}} Hirade, Nishinura 1007-1, 1008-1 |location = {{coord|36|43|22|N|138|15|01|E|scale:20000}} |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |Construction Period = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Dimensions = Total length: approx. 52m; Rear circle diameter: approx. 31m |Buried Person = Unknown |Artifacts = Gridded {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} ({{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}) |Historic Site Designation = Town (formerly village) designated in [[1980]] (Showa 55) }} '''Kōshinzuka Kofun''' (庚申塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Iizuna Town|ja|飯綱町}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}.
The mound is named "Kōshinzuka" because an ancient {{ill|Kōshin-tō|ja|庚申塔}} was enshrined on it. In [[1933]] (Showa 8), Eiji Kuriiwa conducted an investigation and identified the site as a keyhole-shaped burial mound.
The mound, believed to have been constructed in the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, yielded artifacts including gridded {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (cylindrical Haniwa), marking it as the sixth example of such finds in Japan.
== Overview ==
* Shape: {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} * Dimensions: * Total length: approx. 52m * Rear circle diameter: approx. 31m * Rear circle height: 3.5m * Front projection width: approx. 15–17m * Front projection height: 1.5m
<gallery widths="200px"> 庚申塚古墳２.jpg|Close view of Kōshinzuka Kofun 庚申塚古墳３.jpg|Stone monument 庚申塚古墳４.jpg|Kōshin-tō </gallery>
== location ==
* Nagano Prefecture, Kamiminochi District, {{ill|Iizuna Town|ja|飯綱町}}, Hirade, Nishinura 1007-1, 1008-1
== Transportation Access ==
* Nearest Station: {{ill|Shinano Railway|ja|しなの鉄道}} {{ill|Mure Station|ja|牟礼駅}} (Approx. 8 minutes by car)
== References ==
* Mure Village History and School History Compilation Committee, *Mure Village History* (Vol. 1), pp. 419–420, 817.
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Tankakyō|ja|丹霞郷}} * {{ill|List of Burial Mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}

revision.text:
{{Multiple Issues | Refs Needed = June 2023 | Single Source = June 2023 }} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kōshinzuka Kofun |image = [[File:庚申塚古墳１.jpg|250px|Full view of Kōshinzuka Kofun]] |Address = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}{{ill|Iizuna Town|ja|飯綱町}} Hirade, Nishinura 1007-1, 1008-1 |location = {{coord|36|43|22|N|138|15|01|E|scale:20000}} |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |Construction Period = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Dimensions = Total length: approx. 52m; Rear circle diameter: approx. 31m |Buried Person = Unknown |Artifacts = Gridded {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} ({{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}) |Historic Site Designation = Town (formerly village) designated in [[1980]] (Showa 55) }} '''Kōshinzuka Kofun''' (庚申塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Iizuna Town|ja|飯綱町}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}.
The mound is named "Kōshinzuka" because an ancient {{ill|Kōshin-tō|ja|庚申塔}} was enshrined on it. In [[1933]] (Showa 8), Eiji Kuriiwa conducted an investigation and identified the site as a keyhole-shaped burial mound.
The mound, believed to have been constructed in the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, yielded artifacts including gridded {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (cylindrical Haniwa), marking it as the sixth example of such finds in Japan.
== Overview ==
* Shape: {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} * Dimensions: * Total length: approx. 52m * Rear circle diameter: approx. 31m * Rear circle height: 3.5m * Front projection width: approx. 15–17m * Front projection height: 1.5m
<gallery widths="200px"> 庚申塚古墳２.jpg|Close view of Kōshinzuka Kofun 庚申塚古墳３.jpg|Stone monument 庚申塚古墳４.jpg|Kōshin-tō </gallery>
== location ==
* Nagano Prefecture, Kamiminochi District, {{ill|Iizuna Town|ja|飯綱町}}, Hirade, Nishinura 1007-1, 1008-1
== Transportation Access ==
* Nearest Station: {{ill|Shinano Railway|ja|しなの鉄道}} {{ill|Mure Station|ja|牟礼駅}} (Approx. 8 minutes by car)
== References ==
* Mure Village History and School History Compilation Committee, *Mure Village History* (Vol. 1), pp. 419–420, 817.
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Tankakyō|ja|丹霞郷}} * {{ill|List of Burial Mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}

{{Multiple Issues | Refs Needed = June 2023 | Single Source = June 2023 }} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kōshinzuka Kofun |image = [[File:庚申塚古墳１.jpg|250px|Full view of Kōshinzuka Kofun]] |Address = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}{{ill|Iizuna Town|ja|飯綱町}} Hirade, Nishinura 1007-1, 1008-1 |location = {{coord|36|43|22|N|138|15|01|E|scale:20000}} |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |Construction Period = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Dimensions = Total length: approx. 52m; Rear circle diameter: approx. 31m |Buried Person = Unknown |Artifacts = Gridded {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} ({{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}) |Historic Site Designation = Town (formerly village) designated in [[1980]] (Showa 55) }} '''Kōshinzuka Kofun''' (庚申塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Iizuna Town|ja|飯綱町}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}.
The mound is named "Kōshinzuka" because an ancient {{ill|Kōshin-tō|ja|庚申塔}} was enshrined on it. In [[1933]] (Showa 8), Eiji Kuriiwa conducted an investigation and identified the site as a keyhole-shaped burial mound.
The mound, believed to have been constructed in the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, yielded artifacts including gridded {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (cylindrical Haniwa), marking it as the sixth example of such finds in Japan.
== Overview ==
* Shape: {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} * Dimensions: * Total length: approx. 52m * Rear circle diameter: approx. 31m * Rear circle height: 3.5m * Front projection width: approx. 15–17m * Front projection height: 1.5m
<gallery widths="200px"> 庚申塚古墳２.jpg|Close view of Kōshinzuka Kofun 庚申塚古墳３.jpg|Stone monument 庚申塚古墳４.jpg|Kōshin-tō </gallery>

revision.text:
{{Multiple Issues | Refs Needed = June 2023 | Single Source = June 2023 }} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kōshinzuka Kofun |image = [[File:庚申塚古墳１.jpg|250px|Full view of Kōshinzuka Kofun]] |Address = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}{{ill|Iizuna Town|ja|飯綱町}} Hirade, Nishinura 1007-1, 1008-1 |location = {{coord|36|43|22|N|138|15|01|E|scale:20000}} |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |Construction Period = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Dimensions = Total length: approx. 52m; Rear circle diameter: approx. 31m |Buried Person = Unknown |Artifacts = Gridded {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} ({{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}) |Historic Site Designation = Town (formerly village) designated in [[1980]] (Showa 55) }} '''Kōshinzuka Kofun''' (庚申塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Iizuna Town|ja|飯綱町}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}.
The mound is named "Kōshinzuka" because an ancient {{ill|Kōshin-tō|ja|庚申塔}} was enshrined on it. In [[1933]] (Showa 8), Eiji Kuriiwa conducted an investigation and identified the site as a keyhole-shaped burial mound.
The mound, believed to have been constructed in the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, yielded artifacts including gridded {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (cylindrical Haniwa), marking it as the sixth example of such finds in Japan.
== Overview ==
* Shape: {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} * Dimensions: * Total length: approx. 52m * Rear circle diameter: approx. 31m * Rear circle height: 3.5m * Front projection width: approx. 15–17m * Front projection height: 1.5m
<gallery widths="200px"> 庚申塚古墳２.jpg|Close view of Kōshinzuka Kofun 庚申塚古墳３.jpg|Stone monument 庚申塚古墳４.jpg|Kōshin-tō </gallery>
== location ==
* Nagano Prefecture, Kamiminochi District, {{ill|Iizuna Town|ja|飯綱町}}, Hirade, Nishinura 1007-1, 1008-1
== Transportation Access ==
* Nearest Station: {{ill|Shinano Railway|ja|しなの鉄道}} {{ill|Mure Station|ja|牟礼駅}} (Approx. 8 minutes by car)
== References ==
* Mure Village History and School History Compilation Committee, *Mure Village History* (Vol. 1), pp. 419–420, 817.
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Tankakyō|ja|丹霞郷}} * {{ill|List of Burial Mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}

revision.text:
{{Multiple Issues | Refs Needed = June 2023 | Single Source = June 2023 }} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kōshinzuka Kofun |image = [[File:庚申塚古墳１.jpg|250px|Full view of Kōshinzuka Kofun]] |Address = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}{{ill|Iizuna Town|ja|飯綱町}} Hirade, Nishinura 1007-1, 1008-1 |location = {{coord|36|43|22|N|138|15|01|E|scale:20000}} |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |Construction Period = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Dimensions = Total length: approx. 52m; Rear circle diameter: approx. 31m |Buried Person = Unknown |Artifacts = Gridded {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} ({{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}) |Historic Site Designation = Town (formerly village) designated in [[1980]] (Showa 55) }} '''Kōshinzuka Kofun''' (庚申塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Iizuna Town|ja|飯綱町}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}.
The mound is named "Kōshinzuka" because an ancient {{ill|Kōshin-tō|ja|庚申塔}} was enshrined on it. In [[1933]] (Showa 8), Eiji Kuriiwa conducted an investigation and identified the site as a keyhole-shaped burial mound.
The mound, believed to have been constructed in the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, yielded artifacts including gridded {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (cylindrical Haniwa), marking it as the sixth example of such finds in Japan.
== Overview ==
* Shape: {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} * Dimensions: * Total length: approx. 52m * Rear circle diameter: approx. 31m * Rear circle height: 3.5m * Front projection width: approx. 15–17m * Front projection height: 1.5m
<gallery widths="200px"> 庚申塚古墳２.jpg|Close view of Kōshinzuka Kofun 庚申塚古墳３.jpg|Stone monument 庚申塚古墳４.jpg|Kōshin-tō </gallery>
== location ==
* Nagano Prefecture, Kamiminochi District, {{ill|Iizuna Town|ja|飯綱町}}, Hirade, Nishinura 1007-1, 1008-1
== Transportation Access ==
* Nearest Station: {{ill|Shinano Railway|ja|しなの鉄道}} {{ill|Mure Station|ja|牟礼駅}} (Approx. 8 minutes by car)
== References ==
* Mure Village History and School History Compilation Committee, *Mure Village History* (Vol. 1), pp. 419–420, 817.
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Tankakyō|ja|丹霞郷}} * {{ill|List of Burial Mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}

{{Multiple Issues | Refs Needed = June 2023 | Single Source = June 2023 }} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kōshinzuka Kofun |image = [[File:庚申塚古墳１.jpg|250px|Full view of Kōshinzuka Kofun]] |Address = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}{{ill|Iizuna Town|ja|飯綱町}} Hirade, Nishinura 1007-1, 1008-1 |location = {{coord|36|43|22|N|138|15|01|E|scale:20000}} |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |Construction Period = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Dimensions = Total length: approx. 52m; Rear circle diameter: approx. 31m |Buried Person = Unknown |Artifacts = Gridded {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} ({{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}) |Historic Site Designation = Town (formerly village) designated in [[1980]] (Showa 55) }} '''Kōshinzuka Kofun''' (庚申塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Iizuna Town|ja|飯綱町}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}.
The mound is named "Kōshinzuka" because an ancient {{ill|Kōshin-tō|ja|庚申塔}} was enshrined on it. In [[1933]] (Showa 8), Eiji Kuriiwa conducted an investigation and identified the site as a keyhole-shaped burial mound.
The mound, believed to have been constructed in the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, yielded artifacts including gridded {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (cylindrical Haniwa), marking it as the sixth example of such finds in Japan.
== Overview ==
* Shape: {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} * Dimensions: * Total length: approx. 52m * Rear circle diameter: approx. 31m * Rear circle height: 3.5m * Front projection width: approx. 15–17m * Front projection height: 1.5m
<gallery widths="200px"> 庚申塚古墳２.jpg|Close view of Kōshinzuka Kofun 庚申塚古墳３.jpg|Stone monument 庚申塚古墳４.jpg|Kōshin-tō </gallery>
== location ==
* Nagano Prefecture, Kamiminochi District, {{ill|Iizuna Town|ja|飯綱町}}, Hirade, Nishinura 1007-1, 1008-1
== Transportation Access ==
* Nearest Station: {{ill|Shinano Railway|ja|しなの鉄道}} {{ill|Mure Station|ja|牟礼駅}} (Approx. 8 minutes by car)
== References ==
* Mure Village History and School History Compilation Committee, *Mure Village History* (Vol. 1), pp. 419–420, 817.
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Tankakyō|ja|丹霞郷}} * {{ill|List of Burial Mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}

revision.text:
{{Multiple Issues | Refs Needed = June 2023 | Single Source = June 2023 }} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kōshinzuka Kofun |image = [[File:庚申塚古墳１.jpg|250px|Full view of Kōshinzuka Kofun]] |Aqqress = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}{{ill|Iizuna Town|ja|飯綱町}} Hirade, Nishinura 1007-1, 1008-1 |location = {{coord|36|43|22|N|138|15|01|E|scale:20000}} |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |Construction Period = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Dimensions = Total length: approx. 52m; Rear circle diameter: approx. 31m |Buried Person = Unknown |Artifacts = Griqqed {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} ({{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}) |Historic Site Designation = Town (formerly village) designated in [[1980]] (Showa 55) }} '''Kōshinzuka Kofun''' (庚申塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Iizuna Town|ja|飯綱町}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}.
The mound is named "Kōshinzuka" because an ancient {{ill|Kōshin-tō|ja|庚申塔}} was enshrined on it. In [[1933]] (Showa 8), Eiji Kuriiwa conducted an investigation and identified the site as a keyhole-shaped burial mound.
The mound, believed to have been constructed in the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, yielded artifacts including griqqed {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (cylindrical Haniwa), marking it as the sixth example of such finds in Japan.
== Overview ==
* Shape: {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} * Dimensions: * Total length: approx. 52m * Rear circle diameter: approx. 31m * Rear circle height: 3.5m * Front projection width: approx. 15–17m * Front projection height: 1.5m
<gallery widths="200px"> 庚申塚古墳２.jpg|Close view of Kōshinzuka Kofun 庚申塚古墳３.jpg|Stone monument 庚申塚古墳４.jpg|Kōshin-tō </gallery>
== location ==
* Nagano Prefecture, Kamiminochi District, {{ill|Iizuna Town|ja|飯綱町}}, Hirade, Nishinura 1007-1, 1008-1
== Transportation Access ==
* Nearest Station: {{ill|Shinano Railway|ja|しなの鉄道}} {{ill|Mure Station|ja|牟礼駅}} (Approx. 8 minutes by car)
== References ==
* Mure Village History and School History Compilation Committee, *Mure Village History* (Vol. 1), pp. 419–420, 817.
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Tankakyō|ja|丹霞郷}} * {{ill|List of Burial Mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}

{{Multiple Issues | Refs Needed = June 2023 | Single Source = June 2023 }} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kōshinzuka Kofun |image = [[File:庚申塚古墳１.jpg|250px|Full view of Kōshinzuka Kofun]] |Aqqress = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}{{ill|Iizuna Town|ja|飯綱町}} Hirade, Nishinura 1007-1, 1008-1 |location = {{coord|36|43|22|N|138|15|01|E|scale:20000}} |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |Construction Period = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Dimensions = Total length: approx. 52m; Rear circle diameter: approx. 31m |Buried Person = Unknown |Artifacts = Griqqed {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} ({{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}) |Historic Site Designation = Town (formerly village) designated in [[1980]] (Showa 55) }} '''Kōshinzuka Kofun''' (庚申塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Iizuna Town|ja|飯綱町}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}.
The mound is named "Kōshinzuka" because an ancient {{ill|Kōshin-tō|ja|庚申塔}} was enshrined on it. In [[1933]] (Showa 8), Eiji Kuriiwa conducted an investigation and identified the site as a keyhole-shaped Kofun.
The mound, believed to have been constructed in the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, yielded artifacts including griqqed {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (cylindrical Haniwa), marking it as the sixth example of such finds in Japan.
== Overview ==
* Shape: {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} * Dimensions: * Total length: approx. 52m * Rear circle diameter: approx. 31m * Rear circle height: 3.5m * Front projection width: approx. 15–17m * Front projection height: 1.5m
<gallery widths="200px"> 庚申塚古墳２.jpg|Close view of Kōshinzuka Kofun 庚申塚古墳３.jpg|Stone monument 庚申塚古墳４.jpg|Kōshin-tō </gallery>
== location ==
* Nagano Prefecture, Kamiminochi District, {{ill|Iizuna Town|ja|飯綱町}}, Hirade, Nishinura 1007-1, 1008-1
== Transportation Access ==
* Nearest Station: {{ill|Shinano Railway|ja|しなの鉄道}} {{ill|Mure Station|ja|牟礼駅}} (Approx. 8 minutes by car)
== References ==
* Mure Village History and School History Compilation Committee, *Mure Village History* (Vol. 1), pp. 419–420, 817.
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Tankakyō|ja|丹霞郷}} * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}

revision.text:
{{Multiple Issues | Refs Needed = June 2023 | Single Source = June 2023 }} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Kōshinzuka Kofun |image = [[File:庚申塚古墳１.jpg|250px|Full view of Kōshinzuka Kofun]] |A11ress = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}{{ill|Iizuna Town|ja|飯綱町}} Hirade, Nishinura 1007-1, 1008-1 |location = {{coord|36|43|22|N|138|15|01|E|scale:20000}} |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |Construction Period = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Dimensions = Total length: approx. 52m; Rear circle diameter: approx. 31m |Buried Person = Unknown |Artifacts = Gri11ed {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} ({{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}) |Historic Site Designation = Town (formerly village) designated in [[1980]] (Showa 55) }} '''Kōshinzuka Kofun''' (庚申塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Iizuna Town|ja|飯綱町}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}.
The mound is named "Kōshinzuka" because an ancient {{ill|Kōshin-tō|ja|庚申塔}} was enshrined on it. In [[1933]] (Showa 8), Eiji Kuriiwa conducted an investigation and identified the site as a keyhole-shaped Kofun.
The mound, believed to have been constructed in the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, yielded artifacts including gri11ed {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (cylindrical Haniwa), marking it as the sixth example of such finds in Japan.
== Overview ==
* Shape: {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} * Dimensions: ** Total length: approx. 52m ** Rear circle diameter: approx. 31m ** Rear circle height: 3.5m ** Front projection width: approx. 15–17m ** Front projection height: 1.5m
<gallery widths="200px"> 庚申塚古墳２.jpg|Close view of Kōshinzuka Kofun 庚申塚古墳３.jpg|Stone monument 庚申塚古墳４.jpg|Kōshin-tō </gallery>
== location ==
* Nagano Prefecture, Kamiminochi District, {{ill|Iizuna Town|ja|飯綱町}}, Hirade, Nishinura 1007-1, 1008-1
== Transportation Access ==
* Nearest Station: {{ill|Shinano Railway|ja|しなの鉄道}} {{ill|Mure Station|ja|牟礼駅}} (Approx. 8 minutes by car)
== References ==
* Mure Village History and School History Compilation Committee, *Mure Village History* (Vol. 1), pp. 419–420, 817.
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Tankakyō|ja|丹霞郷}} * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}

revision.text:
{{Multiple Issues | Refs Needed = June 2023 | Single Source = June 2023 }} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Kōshinzuka Kofun |image = [[File:庚申塚古墳１.jpg|250px|Full view of Kōshinzuka Kofun]] |A11ress = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}{{ill|Iizuna Town|ja|飯綱町}} Hirade, Nishinura 1007-1, 1008-1 |location = {{coord|36|43|22|N|138|15|01|E|scale:20000}} |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |Construction Period = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Dimensions = Total length: approx. 52m; Rear circle diameter: approx. 31m |Buried Person = Unknown |Artifacts = Gri11ed {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} ({{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}) |Historic Site Designation = Town (formerly village) designated in [[1980]] (Showa 55) }} '''Kōshinzuka Kofun''' (庚申塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Iizuna Town|ja|飯綱町}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}.
The mound is named "Kōshinzuka" because an ancient {{ill|Kōshin-tō|ja|庚申塔}} was enshrined on it. In [[1933]] (Showa 8), Eiji Kuriiwa conducted an investigation and identified the site as a keyhole-shaped Kofun.
The mound, believed to have been constructed in the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, yielded artifacts including gri11ed {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (cylindrical Haniwa), marking it as the sixth example of such finds in Japan.
== Overview ==
* Shape: {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} * Dimensions: ** Total length: approx. 52m ** Rear circle diameter: approx. 31m ** Rear circle height: 3.5m ** Front projection width: approx. 15–17m ** Front projection height: 1.5m
<gallery widths="200px"> 庚申塚古墳２.jpg|Close view of Kōshinzuka Kofun 庚申塚古墳３.jpg|Stone monument 庚申塚古墳４.jpg|Kōshin-tō </gallery>
== location ==
* Nagano Prefecture, Kamiminochi District, {{ill|Iizuna Town|ja|飯綱町}}, Hirade, Nishinura 1007-1, 1008-1
== Transportation Access ==
* Nearest Station: {{ill|Shinano Railway|ja|しなの鉄道}} {{ill|Mure Station|ja|牟礼駅}} (Approx. 8 minutes by car)
== References ==
* Mure Village History and School History Compilation Committee, *Mure Village History* (Vol. 1), pp. 419–420, 817.
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Tankakyō|ja|丹霞郷}} * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Multiple Issues | Refs Needed = June 2023 | Single Source = June 2023 }} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Kōshinzuka Kofun |image = [[File:庚申塚古墳１.jpg|250px|Full view of Kōshinzuka Kofun]] |A11ress = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}{{ill|Iizuna Town|ja|飯綱町}} Hirade, Nishinura 1007-1, 1008-1 |location = {{coord|36|43|22|N|138|15|01|E|scale:20000}} |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |Construction Period = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Dimensions = Total length: approx. 52m; Rear circle diameter: approx. 31m |Buried Person = Unknown |Artifacts = Gri11ed {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} ({{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}) |Historic Site Designation = Town (formerly village) designated in [[1980]] (Showa 55) }} '''Kōshinzuka Kofun''' (庚申塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Iizuna Town|ja|飯綱町}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}.
The mound is named "Kōshinzuka" because an ancient {{ill|Kōshin-tō|ja|庚申塔}} was enshrined on it. In [[1933]] (Showa 8), Eiji Kuriiwa conducted an investigation and identified the site as a keyhole-shaped Kofun.
The mound, believed to have been constructed in the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, yielded artifacts including gri11ed {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (cylindrical Haniwa), marking it as the sixth example of such finds in Japan.
== Overview ==
* Shape: {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} * Dimensions: ** Total length: approx. 52m ** Rear circle diameter: approx. 31m ** Rear circle height: 3.5m ** Front projection width: approx. 15–17m ** Front projection height: 1.5m
<gallery widths="200px"> 庚申塚古墳２.jpg|Close view of Kōshinzuka Kofun 庚申塚古墳３.jpg|Stone monument 庚申塚古墳４.jpg|Kōshin-tō </gallery>
== location ==
* Nagano Prefecture, Kamiminochi District, {{ill|Iizuna Town|ja|飯綱町}}, Hirade, Nishinura 1007-1, 1008-1
== Transportation Access ==
* Nearest Station: {{ill|Shinano Railway|ja|しなの鉄道}} {{ill|Mure Station|ja|牟礼駅}} (Approx. 8 minutes by car)
== References ==
* Mure Village History and School History Compilation Committee, *Mure Village History* (Vol. 1), pp. 419–420, 817.
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Tankakyō|ja|丹霞郷}} * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|Artifacts" to "|burial goods"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Multiple Issues | Refs Needed = June 2023 | Single Source = June 2023 }} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Kōshinzuka Kofun |image = [[File:庚申塚古墳１.jpg|250px|Full view of Kōshinzuka Kofun]] |A11ress = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}{{ill|Iizuna Town|ja|飯綱町}} Hirade, Nishinura 1007-1, 1008-1 |location = {{coord|36|43|22|N|138|15|01|E|scale:20000}} |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |Construction Period = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Dimensions = Total length: approx. 52m; Rear circle diameter: approx. 31m |Buried Person = Unknown |burial goods = Gri11ed {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} ({{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}) |Historic Site Designation = Town (formerly village) designated in [[1980]] (Showa 55) }} '''Kōshinzuka Kofun''' (庚申塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Iizuna Town|ja|飯綱町}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}.
The mound is named "Kōshinzuka" because an ancient {{ill|Kōshin-tō|ja|庚申塔}} was enshrined on it. In [[1933]] (Showa 8), Eiji Kuriiwa conducted an investigation and identified the site as a keyhole-shaped Kofun.
The mound, believed to have been constructed in the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, yielded artifacts including gri11ed {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (cylindrical Haniwa), marking it as the sixth example of such finds in Japan.
== Overview ==
* Shape: {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} * Dimensions: ** Total length: approx. 52m ** Rear circle diameter: approx. 31m ** Rear circle height: 3.5m ** Front projection width: approx. 15–17m ** Front projection height: 1.5m
<gallery widths="200px"> 庚申塚古墳２.jpg|Close view of Kōshinzuka Kofun 庚申塚古墳３.jpg|Stone monument 庚申塚古墳４.jpg|Kōshin-tō </gallery>
== location ==
* Nagano Prefecture, Kamiminochi District, {{ill|Iizuna Town|ja|飯綱町}}, Hirade, Nishinura 1007-1, 1008-1
== Transportation Access ==
* Nearest Station: {{ill|Shinano Railway|ja|しなの鉄道}} {{ill|Mure Station|ja|牟礼駅}} (Approx. 8 minutes by car)
== References ==
* Mure Village History and School History Compilation Committee, *Mure Village History* (Vol. 1), pp. 419–420, 817.== See Also ==
* {{ill|Tankakyō|ja|丹霞郷}} * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}

* Shape: {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} * Dimensions: ** Total length: approx. 52m ** Rear circle diameter: approx. 31m ** Rear circle height: 3.5m ** Front projection width: approx. 15–17m ** Front projection height: 1.5m
<gallery widths="200px"> 庚申塚古墳２.jpg|Close view of Kōshinzuka Kofun 庚申塚古墳３.jpg|Stone monument 庚申塚古墳４.jpg|Kōshin-tō </gallery>
== location ==
* Nagano Prefecture, Kamiminochi District, {{ill|Iizuna Town|ja|飯綱町}}, Hirade, Nishinura 1007-1, 1008-1
== Transportation Access ==
* Nearest Station: {{ill|Shinano Railway|ja|しなの鉄道}} {{ill|Mure Station|ja|牟礼駅}} (Approx. 8 minutes by car)
== References ==
* Mure Village History and School History Compilation Committee, *Mure Village History* (Vol. 1), pp. 419–420, 817.== See Also ==

== Overview ==
* Shape: {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} * Dimensions: ** Total length: approx. 52m ** Rear circle diameter: approx. 31m ** Rear circle height: 3.5m ** Front projection width: approx. 15–17m ** Front projection height: 1.5m

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Multiple Issues | Refs Needed = June 2023 | Single Source = June 2023 }} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Kōshinzuka Kofun |image = [[File:庚申塚古墳１.jpg|250px|Full view of Kōshinzuka Kofun]] |A11ress = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}{{ill|Iizuna Town|ja|飯綱町}} Hirade, Nishinura 1007-1, 1008-1 |location = {{coord|36|43|22|N|138|15|01|E|map_size:20000}} |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |built = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |area = Total length: approx. 52m; Rear circle diameter: approx. 31m |Buried Person = Unknown |burial goods = Gri11ed {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} ({{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}) |Historic Site Designation = Town (formerly village) designated in [[1980]] (Showa 55) }} '''Kōshinzuka Kofun''' (庚申塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Iizuna Town|ja|飯綱町}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}.
The mound is named "Kōshinzuka" because an ancient {{ill|Kōshin-tō|ja|庚申塔}} was enshrined on it. In [[1933]] (Showa 8), Eiji Kuriiwa conducted an investigation and identified the site as a keyhole-shaped Kofun.
The mound, believed to have been constructed in the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, yielded artifacts including gri11ed {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (cylindrical Haniwa), marking it as the sixth example of such finds in Japan.
== Overview ==
* Shape: {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} * area: ** Total length: approx. 52m ** Rear circle diameter: approx. 31m ** Rear circle height: 3.5m ** Front projection width: approx. 15–17m ** Front projection height: 1.5m
<gallery widths="200px"> 庚申塚古墳２.jpg|Close view of Kōshinzuka Kofun 庚申塚古墳３.jpg|Stone monument 庚申塚古墳４.jpg|Kōshin-tō </gallery>
== location ==
* Nagano Prefecture, Kamiminochi District, {{ill|Iizuna Town|ja|飯綱町}}, Hirade, Nishinura 1007-1, 1008-1
== Transportation Access ==
* Nearest Station: {{ill|Shinano Railway|ja|しなの鉄道}} {{ill|Mure Station|ja|牟礼駅}} (Approx. 8 minutes by car)
== References ==
* Mure Village History and School History Compilation Committee, *Mure Village History* (Vol. 1), pp. 419–420, 817.== See Also ==
* {{ill|Tankakyō|ja|丹霞郷}} * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. * Shape: {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} * area: ** Total length: approx. 52m ** Rear circle diameter: approx. 31m ** Rear circle height: 3.5m ** Front projection width: approx. 15–17m ** Front projection height: 1.5m
<gallery widths="200px"> 庚申塚古墳２.jpg|Close view of Kōshinzuka Kofun 庚申塚古墳３.jpg|Stone monument 庚申塚古墳４.jpg|Kōshin-tō </gallery>
== location ==

{{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Multiple Issues | Refs Needed = June 2023 | Single Source = June 2023 }} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Kōshinzuka Kofun |image = [[File:庚申塚古墳１.jpg|250px|Full view of Kōshinzuka Kofun]] |A11ress = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}{{ill|Iizuna Town|ja|飯綱町}} Hirade, Nishinura 1007-1, 1008-1 |location = {{coord|36|43|22|N|138|15|01|E|map_size:20000}} |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |built = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |area = Total length: approx. 52m; Rear circle diameter: approx. 31m |Buried Person = Unknown |burial goods = Gri11ed {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} ({{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}) |Historic Site Designation = Town (formerly village) designated in [[1980]] (Showa 55) }} '''Kōshinzuka Kofun''' (庚申塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Iizuna Town|ja|飯綱町}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}.
The mound is named "Kōshinzuka" because an ancient {{ill|Kōshin-tō|ja|庚申塔}} was enshrined on it. In [[1933]] (Showa 8), Eiji Kuriiwa conducted an investigation and identified the site as a keyhole-shaped Kofun.
The mound, believed to have been constructed in the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, yielded artifacts including gri11ed {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (cylindrical Haniwa), marking it as the sixth example of such finds in Japan.
== Overview ==
* type: {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} * area: ** Total length: approx. 52m ** Rear circle diameter: approx. 31m ** Rear circle height: 3.5m ** Front projection width: approx. 15–17m ** Front projection height: 1.5m
<gallery widths="200px"> 庚申塚古墳２.jpg|Close view of Kōshinzuka Kofun 庚申塚古墳３.jpg|Stone monument 庚申塚古墳４.jpg|Kōshin-tō </gallery>
== location ==
* Nagano Prefecture, Kamiminochi District, {{ill|Iizuna Town|ja|飯綱町}}, Hirade, Nishinura 1007-1, 1008-1
== Transportation Access ==
* Nearest Station: {{ill|Shinano Railway|ja|しなの鉄道}} {{ill|Mure Station|ja|牟礼駅}} (Approx. 8 minutes by car)
== References ==
* Mure Village History and School History Compilation Committee, *Mure Village History* (Vol. 1), pp. 419–420, 817.== See Also ==
* {{ill|Tankakyō|ja|丹霞郷}} * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Multiple Issues | Refs Needed = June 2023 | Single Source = June 2023 }} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Kōshinzuka Kofun |image = [[File:庚申塚古墳１.jpg|250px|Full view of Kōshinzuka Kofun]] |A11ress = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}{{ill|Iizuna Town|ja|飯綱町}} Hirade, Nishinura 1007-1, 1008-1 |location = {{coord|36|43|22|N|138|15|01|E|map_size:20000}} |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |built = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |area = Total length: approx. 52m; Rear circle diameter: approx. 31m |Buried Person = Unknown |burial goods = Gri11ed {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} ({{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}) |Historic Site Designation = Town (formerly village) designated in [[1980]] (Showa 55) }} '''Kōshinzuka Kofun''' (庚申塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Iizuna Town|ja|飯綱町}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}.
The mound is named "Kōshinzuka" because an ancient {{ill|Kōshin-tō|ja|庚申塔}} was enshrined on it. In [[1933]] (Showa 8), Eiji Kuriiwa conducted an investigation and identified the site as a keyhole-shaped Kofun.
The mound, believed to have been constructed in the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, yielded artifacts including gri11ed {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (cylindrical Haniwa), marking it as the sixth example of such finds in Japan.
== Overview ==
* type: {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} * area: ** Total length: approx. 52m ** Rear circle diameter: approx. 31m ** Rear circle height: 3.5m ** Front projection width: approx. 15–17m ** Front projection height: 1.5m
<gallery widths="200px"> 庚申塚古墳２.jpg|Close view of Kōshinzuka Kofun 庚申塚古墳３.jpg|Stone monument 庚申塚古墳４.jpg|Kōshin-tō </gallery>
== location ==
* Nagano Prefecture, Kamiminochi District, {{ill|Iizuna Town|ja|飯綱町}}, Hirade, Nishinura 1007-1, 1008-1
== Transportation Access ==
* Nearest Station: {{ill|Shinano Railway|ja|しなの鉄道}} {{ill|Mure Station|ja|牟礼駅}} (Approx. 8 minutes by car)
== References ==
* Mure Village History and School History Compilation Committee, *Mure Village History* (Vol. 1), pp. 419–420, 817.== See Also ==
* {{ill|Tankakyō|ja|丹霞郷}} * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}

<gallery widths="200px"> 庚申塚古墳２.jpg|Close view of Kōshinzuka Kofun 庚申塚古墳３.jpg|Stone monument 庚申塚古墳４.jpg|Kōshin-tō </gallery>
== location ==
* Nagano Prefecture, Kamiminochi District, {{ill|Iizuna Town|ja|飯綱町}}, Hirade, Nishinura 1007-1, 1008-1
== Transportation Access ==
* Nearest Station: {{ill|Shinano Railway|ja|しなの鉄道}} {{ill|Mure Station|ja|牟礼駅}} (Approx. 8 minutes by car)
== References ==
* Mure Village History and School History Compilation Committee, *Mure Village History* (Vol. 1), pp. 419–420, 817.== See Also ==

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Multiple Issues | Refs Needed = June 2023 | Single Source = June 2023 }} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Kōshinzuka Kofun |image = [[File:庚申塚古墳１.jpg|250px|Full view of Kōshinzuka Kofun]] |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}{{ill|Iizuna Town|ja|飯綱町}} Hirade, Nishinura 1007-1, 1008-1 |location = {{coord|36|43|22|N|138|15|01|E|map_size:20000}} |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |built = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |area = Total length: approx. 52m; Rear circle diameter: approx. 31m |Buried Person = Unknown |burial goods = Gri11ed {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} ({{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}) |other_designation Designation = Town (formerly village) designated in [[1980]] (Showa 55) }} '''Kōshinzuka Kofun''' (庚申塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Iizuna Town|ja|飯綱町}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}.
The mound is named "Kōshinzuka" because an ancient {{ill|Kōshin-tō|ja|庚申塔}} was enshrined on it. In [[1933]] (Showa 8), Eiji Kuriiwa conducted an investigation and identified the site as a keyhole-shaped Kofun.
The mound, believed to have been constructed in the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, yielded artifacts including gri11ed {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (cylindrical Haniwa), marking it as the sixth example of such finds in Japan.
== Overview ==
* type: {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} * area: ** Total length: approx. 52m ** Rear circle diameter: approx. 31m ** Rear circle height: 3.5m ** Front projection width: approx. 15–17m ** Front projection height: 1.5m
<gallery widths="200px"> 庚申塚古墳２.jpg|Close view of Kōshinzuka Kofun 庚申塚古墳３.jpg|Stone monument 庚申塚古墳４.jpg|Kōshin-tō </gallery>
== location ==
* Nagano Prefecture, Kamiminochi District, {{ill|Iizuna Town|ja|飯綱町}}, Hirade, Nishinura 1007-1, 1008-1
== Transportation Access ==
* Nearest Station: {{ill|Shinano Railway|ja|しなの鉄道}} {{ill|Mure Station|ja|牟礼駅}} (Approx. 8 minutes by car)
== References ==
* Mure Village History and School History Compilation Committee, *Mure Village History* (Vol. 1), pp. 419–420, 817.== See Also ==
* {{ill|Tankakyō|ja|丹霞郷}} * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}

The mound is named "Kōshinzuka" because an ancient {{ill|Kōshin-tō|ja|庚申塔}} was enshrined on it. In [[1933]] (Showa 8), Eiji Kuriiwa conducted an investigation and identified the site as a keyhole-shaped Kofun.
The mound, believed to have been constructed in the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, yielded artifacts including gri11ed {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (cylindrical Haniwa), marking it as the sixth example of such finds in Japan.
== Overview ==
* type: {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} * area: ** Total length: approx. 52m ** Rear circle diameter: approx. 31m ** Rear circle height: 3.5m ** Front projection width: approx. 15–17m ** Front projection height: 1.5m
